feedback.text: Hello, I'd like to bring attention to a topic that's been bothering many people internationally, and specifically, more in Europe for a while now. The topic is about preserving the right to access bought video game products when they've been shut down from online services. In my opinion, Digital Fairness Act should include video games as many people are impacted by the shut-down of said products and are essentially being robbed of their games, due to companies whims. Consumers should be entitled to their products that they spent money on. The companies shouldn't be able to entirely bar access to paid products consumers have paid for, but leave games in a state they can be access offline, or leave tools that will let communities host or maintain said games themselves. Please take into consideration this proposal, review it, and act on it. Consumer rights should be protected, so their purchased products. Best regards.
